sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 45:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 45:1
items.html: The Sium of a upper degree is called “a tail for the lions.” “Tail” implies Sof and Sium. When the point of Sium descends to the lower degree, it becomes the root phase there, and it is called the “head of the foxes,” since in relation to the ten Sefirot of the upper degree, the ten Sefirot in the lower one are regarded as “foxes” compared to “lions.” (Part 3, Chapter 7, item 5)
